chief_complaint: A 2-month-old male infant presented with severe diabetic ketoacidosis.
history: The infant was born to a consanguineous marriage and presented to the Pediatric Intensive Care Unit (PICU) with severe hyperglycemia, pH 6.984, and ketones in urine. Genomic sequencing analyses detected a novel homozygous missense variation in the Exon 11 of the EIF2AK3 gene.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: not provided.
labs: Genomic DNA sequencing analyses: novel homozygous missense variation in the Exon 11 of the EIF2AK3 gene (p.Gly602Val). Blood glucose: severe hyperglycemia. pH: 6.984. Ketones in urine: present.
correct_diagnosis: Wolcott-Rallison syndrome due to a novel homozygous missense variation (p.Gly602Val) in the exon 11 of EIF2AK3 gene
differentials: Wolcott-Rallison syndrome due to a novel homozygous missense variation (p.Gly602Val) in the exon 11 of EIF2AK3 gene
correct_ranking: Wolcott-Rallison syndrome due to a novel homozygous missense variation (p.Gly602Val) in the exon 11 of EIF2AK3 gene
